name: João Paulo International School

location.address: Rua de S. Frutuoso n.º 40,, , Dume (S. Martinho) 4700-085 Braga, , Portugal, Braga | 7 Fontes: Ages 3 to 11, Address:, Rua José Gabriel Bacelar n.º 154,, , 4710 Braga, , Portugal, Braga | Dume: Ages 11 to 18, Address:, Rua de S. Frutuoso n.º 40,, , Dume (S. Martinho) 4700-085 Braga, , Portugal, Students in Joao Paulo International School choose either 100% Portuguese or 100% English curriculum., School details, We have organised the information available for this school into 10 sections and 39 questions. Click or tap on the name of each section to read more., Nationalities, Number of students, Approximately 2000 students., Number of nationalities represented in the school, Around 19 nationalities., Most common nationality, Portuguese., Languages, Native English and Portuguese speaking teachers, Yes. Most teachers are native Portuguese speakers. The school also employs qualified native and near-native English teachers., Language support for students not fluent in English and Portuguese, Yes. The school provides Portuguese as a Foreign Language (PFL) support classes when necessary., Additional language classes offered by the school, Spanish, German and French are offered, depending on grade level and student demand., Classroom, Max. number of students per class, Up to 25 students., Average number of students per class, Around 23 students., Does the school employ teaching assistants?, Yes, especially in early years education and in classes that require additional learning support., Use of technology in the classroom, Classrooms are equipped with computers, interactive whiteboards or projectors, and digital learning platforms to support teaching, research, assessment, and communication., From Year 7 onwards, the school implements the Apple Education Project, in which students use iPads as a core learning tool across different subjects, promoting digital literacy, creativity, collaboration, and responsible use of technology., Is the school ready to use virtual classrooms when needed? (e.g. teaching lessons online if the school is closed, e.g. during COVID-19 closures), Yes. The school is fully prepared to deliver online lessons through video-conferencing platforms and digital learning environments in emergency situations., Do students practice religion at the school? What religion?, Yes. The school follows a Catholic educational tradition., How does the school accommodate non-religious students or students of a different religion?, The school promotes respect and inclusion. Participation in religious activities is not compulsory, and students of other beliefs are fully respected and integrated., Academics, External examinations or assessments available, Students complete the Portuguese national examinations. The school also prepares students for international programmes, including the International Baccalaureate (IB) and IGCSEs, depending on the academic pathway., Do teachers assign homework to their students?, Yes., Approximate hours of homework given, Depending on the students’ age and grade level, Student support, Dedicated staff/programs for students with special learning needs, Yes., Learning support offered, The school provides learning support teachers, individualized education plans, differentiated instruction strategies, and close cooperation with families and external specialists when required., The school supports gifted, able and talented students, Through curriculum enrichment, project-based learning, academic challenges, participation in competitions, and personalised learning pathways., Student access to education psychologist, Yes., Admissions, Entry evaluation for students, Yes., Brief description of entry evaluation required, The evaluation may include academic testing, language assessment, and an interview with the student and parents., Waiting list, Yes, for certain year groups., Deadline for registration (new academic year), There is no set deadline for registration for the new academic year. Admissions are subject to availability., Students can join after academic year begins, Yes, subject to availability., School day, School start time, At 8AM, School finish time, At 8PM, Supervised care before/after school, Yes., School bus service available, Yes, including intercity routes., School provided lunches, Yes, in the school cafeteria., Food alternatives for special dietary needs (ie. vegan, kosher, halal etc), Yes, including vegetarian, vegan, allergy-friendly and religious dietary options., Uniform required, Yes., Extracurricular activities, Extracurricular activities or clubs offered, Sports clubs, ballet, music, choir, drama, robotics, visual arts, languages, study support, and social responsibility projects., Sports activities included, Football, volleyball, basketball, athletics, gymnastics and other age-appropriate physical education activities., Sports teams or sport competitions available for students, Yes., Facilities, Facilities at the school, Library, science laboratories, ICT rooms, art and music rooms, auditorium, cafeteria, study areas and pastoral support spaces., Sports facilities at the school, Indoor sports hall, outdoor football fields, multipurpose courts and playground areas., About the school, Qualities and characteristics best defining the school, Colégio João Paulo II is defined by academic excellence, strong human and Christian values, close teacher-student relationships, a safe and welcoming environment, and a commitment to the integral development of each student., Teaching approach of the school, The school adopts a student-centred and values-based educational approach, combining high academic standards with personal, social and ethical development. Teaching emphasizes critical thinking, autonomy, creativity, digital literacy and responsibility., Students at João Paulo International School choose either a 100% Portuguese or a 100% English curriculum. Through initiatives such as the Apple Education Project from Year 7 onwards, the school integrates technology in a meaningful way to enhance learning and prepare students for the challenges of higher education and modern society., Do you know, João Paulo International School, ?, How would you rate it?

languages_taught: depending on grade level and student demand.

name: Prime School

location.address: R. Mestre Neves 20, Sintra - 2710-422 Sintra, Portugal, Estoril Campus: Ages 3 to 18, Address:, R. Antoine de Saint-Éxupery, Estoril, Portugal, Lisbon Campus: Ages 3 to 18, Address:, R. Carlos Lobo de Ávila 4, 1700-099, Lisbon, Portugal, The school's life in pictures, Learn more, School details, We have organised the information available for this school into 11 sections and 44 questions. Click or tap on the name of each section to read more., Nationalities, Number of students, 200 per campus., Number of nationalities represented in the school, +51, Most common nationality, UK, USA, Asia, European and Portuguese., Ratio of local students to international students, 20%, Languages, Native English speaking teachers, Yes most of them., Language support for students not fluent in English, Yes, Prime School have English and Portuguese support classes., Additional language classes offered by the school, Portuguese, French, Spanish, Chinese, and German., Classroom, Max. number of students per class, 15 students., Average number of students per class, 12, Does the school employ teaching assistants?, Yes., Use of technology in the classroom, Each classroom is equipped with a computer and projector, and in addition, we provide an IT room that students can use at their convenience for both studying and leisure activities. The robotic classes are fully equipped with the necessary hardware and software, and for students pursuing a career in aviation, we offer access to a state-of-the-art motion flight simulator., Is the school ready to use virtual classrooms when needed? (e.g. teaching lessons online if the school is closed, e.g. during COVID-19 closures), Indeed, during the last academic period, Prime School implemented an online teaching method in response to the Covid-19 pandemic. The feedback from both students and parents was overwhelmingly positive. For teachers who faced challenges in using technology, our team at Prime School provided IT support to ensure a smooth transition to the online teaching environment., Do students practice religion at the school? What religion?, No., How does the school accommodate non-religious students or students of a different religion?, As an international school, we do not promote any particular religious themes in our curriculum., Academics, External examinations or assessments available, Cambridge International IGCSE, AS & A Level qualifications, Higher National Certificate and Higher National Diploma., Results in these examinations, On average, students who take the Cambridge International IGCSE, AS, and A Level qualifications at our school achieve excellent results. Approximately 60% of students receive an A grade, 40% receive a B grade, and 20% receive a C grade., Do teachers assign homework to their students?, Yes., Approximate hours of homework given, Half an hour per day., Percentage of students who pursue further education post-graduation, 98% of the students.  We also offer Higher National Certificate and Diploma qualifications., Student support, Dedicated staff/programs for students with special learning needs, Yes, we have. We offer a specialised learning system to students with special needs., Learning support offered, Each one is different, so we do not have a standart program, we adapt it to their necessities., The school supports gifted, able and talented students, At Prime School, we are committed to supporting the individual needs of all students, including those who are gifted, able, or talented. We recognize that these students require unique challenges and opportunities to reach their full potential. As such, we offer a range of specialized programs and services to support these students in their academic, social, and emotional growth., Our gifted and talented students benefit from enrichment programs that provide them with opportunities to explore subjects in greater depth, participate in advanced coursework, and engage in research projects. We also offer specialized mentoring programs, individualized learning plans, and access to resources that are tailored to their needs., In addition, we encourage our gifted, able, and talented students to participate in extracurricular activities, clubs, and competitions that align with their interests and talents. We believe that these experiences help to develop their skills, broaden their horizons, and foster a lifelong love of learning., Overall, our school is dedicated to creating an inclusive and supportive learning environment that allows all students to reach their full potential., Student access to education psychologist, Yes., Admissions, Entry evaluation for students, As part of our admission process, we require all prospective students to take an English language assessment and admission exam. This is done on the first week of every school year. This helps us to evaluate their language proficiency and ensure that they are prepared for the academic challenges of our curriculum., Brief description of entry evaluation required, Prime School conducts an evaluation before admitting new students, we typically administer an admission exam that covers various subjects such as Math, Geography, History, Science, English, and more., The purpose of this exam is to assess the student's knowledge base and readiness for the grade level they are seeking admission to., Waiting list, Yes we  have a waiting list for admission to our school., Deadline for registration (new academic year), At our school, there is no specific deadline for registration in a new academic year. We welcome students to enroll at any time during the year, as long as there are available spaces in the relevant grade level., Students can join after academic year begins, Yes., School day, School start time, 08:30, School finish time, 15:30, Supervised care before/after school, While our primary focus is on providing a high-quality academic education, we do offer supervised care for students both before and after the school day. We understand that families have busy schedules and may need additional support, and we aim to provide a safe and structured environment for students during these times. Additionally, we offer a variety of extracurricular activities that are available to students outside of regular school hours, which can provide a fun and engaging way for students to spend their time., School bus service available, Yes, only for Sintra and Estoril, School provided lunches, Yes., Food alternatives for special dietary needs (ie. vegan, kosher, halal etc), Yes., Uniform required, Yes., Extracurricular activities, Extracurricular activities or clubs offered, Flight Simulator, Sailing, Robotics, Music, ICT, Sports, Arts and Drama, Surf, Tennis and Football., Sports activities included, Physical education, Sports teams or sport competitions available for students, Yes, Facilities, Facilities at the school, A state-of-the-art motion flight simulator, Robotic Lab, Science lab, Music/Arts class, Games room, and IT room, library, Sports facilities at the school, Our school features a gymnasium, as well as a court equipped with basketball and football nets, providing ample opportunities for students to engage in physical activity and sports., Boarding, Grades/ages available for boarding, 13 years and above., Boarding facilities at the school, Yes, we are proud to offer a premier boarding school experience that provides students with a nurturing and supportive environment that they can call their home away from home. Our state-of-the-art facilities and dedicated staff provide a safe and comfortable living space for students to thrive both academically and personally. From top-notch academic programs to a range of extracurricular activities, we provide our boarding students with a holistic education that prepares them for success in their future endeavours. We are committed to creating a welcoming community where students can grow, learn, and create lifelong memories., About the school, Qualities and characteristics best defining the school, At Prime School International, we strive to create a dynamic and inclusive learning environment where students can thrive academically, socially, and emotionally. Our dedicated faculty inspires a love for learning while fostering critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and character development. We aim to develop compassionate, responsible, and respectful individuals who make a positive impact in their communities. Our state-of-the-art facilities and technology-rich classrooms provide students with the tools they need to explore their passions, develop their talents, and become well-rounded individuals., But we don't stop there. We are committed to helping our students achieve their academic and career goals. Our experienced guidance counselors work closely with older students to provide individualized support and assistance in choosing their future academic objectives. We monitor and follow up with our students to ensure that they are achieving their goals and receiving the necessary resources to help them succeed. At Prime School International, we believe in providing a holistic education that prepares our students for success in an interconnected and rapidly changing world., Teaching approach of the school, At Prime School International, we use the highly regarded Cambridge curriculum which emphasizes a student-centred approach to learning. Our teachers encourage students to take an active role in their education and promote critical thinking, problem-solving, and independent learning skills. We believe that this approach helps to cultivate lifelong learners who are prepared to succeed in a rapidly changing global society., Learn more, Why choose Prime School?, Estoril Campus, The International Anglo-American School forms part of the Prime School International network, an institution delivering both, Elementary and University Education within a single structure. Each student selects a curriculum tailor-made to meet their educational requirements., As an Anglo-American school, the educational offer is delivered via two curricula, namely;, • Cambridge's British curriculum, • American curriculum, Both curricula are Internationally recognized, preparing students from 3 to 19 years of age for entrance into the world's best Universities.  Students furthermore have the opportunity of pursuing their University courses in our facilities, with Prime International College., Sintra Campus, College Infanta D. Maria de Portugal is an inclusive and popular primary school where excellence is celebrated and everybody is cared for., We form part of the Prime School International network, an institution delivering from Kindergarten to Lower Secondary education for students from 3 to 14 years old. We aim to provide all children with a safe, challenging and stimulating environment, where children can grow personally, socially and academically., We offer an innovative curriculum underpinned by dedicated and imaginative teaching that develops a lifelong sense of purpose and curiosity in learning. To do this we wish to secure a passion for learning as well as an ability to think independently., Lisbon Campus, The Heritage International School forms part of the Prime School International network, an institution delivering both, Elementary and University Education within a single structure. Each student selects a curriculum tailor-made to meet their educational requirements., Cambridge's British curriculum is internationally recognized, preparing students from 3 to 19 years of age for entrance into the world's best Universities.  Students furthermore have the opportunity of pursuing their University courses in our facilities, with Prime International College., Boarding, We present you Prime Boarding - A home away from home  to students from the age of 13.  Located only 10 minutes away from our Estoril campus and 20 minutes away from our Sintra campus., Here, students will feel welcome and accommodated, with transport, meals and a fee that includes, besides the above, school fees, books, uniforms, registration fee and personal accident insurance., As the first International School in the area with a Boarding School, at Prime Boarding students are encouraged to explore who they are and have the opportunity to join in any activity that suits their interests., Learn more, Get in contact with the Admissions team, Admissions Team

name: The British School of Lisbon

location.address: Rua de Alcolena 10, Restelo, 1400-004, Lisbon, Portugal, Cais de Sodré Campus: Ages 3 to 11, Address:, Rua de S. Paulo 89, 1200-427 Lisbon, Portugal, The school's life in pictures, Learn more, School details, We have organised the information available for this school into 10 sections and 36 questions. Click or tap on the name of each section to read more., Nationalities, Number of students, 160, Number of nationalities represented in the school, 30+, Most common nationality, We don't have a most common nationality., Ratio of local students to international students, Mostly international students., Languages, Native English speaking teachers, Yes, Language support for students not fluent in English, Yes, Additional language classes offered by the school, Portuguese, Classroom, Max. number of students per class, 22, Average number of students per class, 18, Does the school employ teaching assistants?, Yes, Use of technology in the classroom, We have the latest Interactive Whiteboards (IWB) in every classroom, plus iPads and latest Apple Macs., Is the school ready to use virtual classrooms when needed? (e.g. teaching lessons online if the school is closed, e.g. during COVID-19 closures), Yes, Do students practice religion at the school? What religion?, No, Academics, External examinations or assessments available, SATs, Do teachers assign homework to their students?, Yes, Approximate hours of homework given, Approximately 1 hour per week., Student support, Dedicated staff/programs for students with special learning needs, Limited, The school supports gifted, able and talented students, Enrichment and extra-curricular sessions., Student access to education psychologist, Yes, Admissions, Entry evaluation for students, Yes, Brief description of entry evaluation required, Evaluations are used to level a child and aid the teachers in finding which things a child excels at or indeed has gaps in., Deadline for registration (new academic year), No deadline, we enrol all year round., Students can join after academic year begins, Yes, School day, School start time, 8:30, School finish time, 16:30, Supervised care before/after school, Yes, School bus service available, Yes, School provided lunches, Yes, Food alternatives for special dietary needs (ie. vegan, kosher, halal etc), Yes, Uniform required, Yes, Extracurricular activities, Extracurricular activities or clubs offered, Clubs vary each term, they are a mix of teachers and outside specialists and can be anything from Karate to Chess Club., Sports activities included, PE lessons are twice weekly, includes swimming lessons., Sports teams or sport competitions available for students, Yes, Facilities, Sports facilities at the school, Large indoor gym area with marked multi purpose area outdoors., About the school, Qualities and characteristics best defining the school, Our school’s mission is to be “A World Leading School,” which means nothing less than having the highest of standards in everything we do. We are unashamedly ambitious with the education we provide, ensuring our children thrive not just academically, but also personally and socially. Every child who attends our school is uniquely special to us and we endeavour to instil, not only a love for learning, but the values and experiences that will remain with them throughout their lives., Teaching approach of the school, The British School of Lisbon is dedicated to the best of traditional educational values, offering a stimulating curriculum with a particular focus on numeracy and literacy from an early age. Our vibrant and enriched extracurricular programme also seeks to expose our children to quality tuition in music, drama, sports, and the arts., Learn more, Why choose The British School of Lisbon?, Learning, The British School of Lisbon’s curriculum is based on the English National Curriculum.  This curriculum is considered by many to be the gold standard as it is both rigorous, respected and recognised around the world.  BSL's curriculum is adapted to allow a more international perspective and includes studies of local culture, language and heritage., The curriculum for England is segmented into phases of Early Years, Primary and Secondary schools., Cais de Sodré Campus, The campus provides a wonderfully bright, modern environment in which students can study and explore.  The campus is located in the heart of Lisbon at Cais do Sodré and students have access to up-to-date and traditional tools, specialist classrooms, central library and more., The school library provides access to collections, services, and spaces sufficient in quality, depth, diversity, and format to support BSL's research and teaching missions., While on campus, teachers take advantage of the school’s wireless internet network and Interactive Whiteboards (IWBs) that enable the presentation of all media types in the classroom., The school has designed practice, rehearsal, and performances spaces to help young artists realise their level of success as music and drama students., Restelo Campus, Our Restelo Campus is located only 12 minutes from our Cais de Sodré Campus and 25 minutes drive from Cascais, located within the OS Belenenses Football Complex., Restelo Campus opened in September 2024, welcoming secondary students. The facilities include bright, spacious classrooms, fully equipped science labs, and a variety of play areas and sports fields for our students to enjoy., Learn more, Get in contact with the Admissions team, Admissions Team
